data.title: EGA supplies CelestiAL solar aluminium to global automotive parts maker Hyundai Mobis
data.description:
Staff Writer

ABU DHABI - Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) today announced an agreement to increase its CelestiAL solar aluminium supply to Hyundai Mobis, the global automotive parts maker.

The agreement is an extension of an existing supply agreement with Hyundai Mobis. EGA began supplying aluminium to Hyundai Mobis in 2015.

Under the new agreement, the volume of CelestiAL supplied to Hyundai Mobis will increase from eight thousand this year to up to 15 thousand tonnes per year by 2026.

EGA and Mobis will explore a long-term agreement beyond 2026 to supply value-added products, including billets, primary foundry alloys and recycled aluminium.

EGA and Hyundai Mobis will also collaborate to innovate exclusive new alloys for automotive applications.

Aluminium is a key metal for the automotive industry due to its lightweight, strength, and corrosion resistance properties. EGA is one of the largest suppliers of foundry alloys to the automotive industry worldwide.

Abdulnasser Bin Kalban, Chief Executive Officer of Emirates Global Aluminium, said, “At EGA, we remain committed to innovation in delivering the highest quality, low-carbon aluminium to our customers. We value our successful partnership with Hyundai Mobis and look forward to building on this collaboration in the years ahead. We appreciate their continued trust in EGA and our world-first CelestiAL solar aluminium.”

Sun Woo Lee, Senior Vice President, Head of Procurement of Hyundai Mobis, said, “With a partnership with EGA, we will proactively respond to global environmental regulations by establishing a green supply chain using low carbon aluminium.”

In 2024, production of CelestiAL solar aluminium grew by 27 percent to 80 thousand tonnes, including eight thousand tonnes of CelestiAL-R further sweetened with recycled content.

EGA is certified to the global standard established by the automotive industry which aims to ensure even more rigorous quality management in the global automotive supply chain.

data.title: REG - Geiger Counter Ltd Geiger Counter - GCS - Share Buyback
data.description:
RNS Number : 8059K Geiger Counter Ltd 30 May 2025  

30 May 2025

GEIGER COUNTER LIMITED

(THE "COMPANY")

Share Buy Back

Geiger Counter Limited announces that on 29 May 2025, it purchased 1,458,670 ordinary shares of no par value ("Ordinary Shares") in the Company at an average price of 40.778 pence per Ordinary Share.

All Ordinary Shares repurchased by the Company under its share repurchase programme are to be held in Treasury. In total, since the Annual General Meeting on 5 March 2025 the Company has repurchased 5,794,698 Ordinary Shares.

Following the purchase, the Company has 127,735,004 Ordinary Shares with voting rights in issue plus 24,939,245 Ordinary Shares held in Treasury.

More information on the Company is available on the website. https://ncim.co.uk/wp/geiger-counter-ltd/

data.title: U.K. to Launch New September 2038 Index-Linked Gilt via Syndication in Week Starting June 9
data.description:
By Miriam Mukuru

The U.K. Debt Management Office plans to launch a new September 2038 index-linked gilt through syndication in the week starting in June 9, subject to demand and market conditions, it said on Friday.

This will be the second syndication by the DMO in the fiscal year 2026; the first was a new long conventional gilt maturing in 2056, launched on May 20.

"Further details of this transaction, including the coupon on the new gilt, will be announced in due course," DMO said.

Write to Miriam Mukuru at miriam.mukuru@wsj.com

data.title: SocGen's Overnight Economic News Summary
data.description:
Societe Generale in its early Friday economic news summary pointed out:

— United States administration mulls stopgap plan to impose tariffs as high as 15% for 150 days (WSJ) after federal appeals court temporarily pauses ruling by Trade Court blocking reciprocal tariffs. Appeals court ordered administration to respond by June 9.

— Bond yields steady after a bearish outside day on Thursday for U.S. Treasuries and Bunds. 10-year UST yield on 100dma (4.40%), support 4.38%-4.34%. Bund support 2.43%. Bearish outside day for signals further downside potential. The seve-year UST auction was solid: stop through 2.2bp, non-dealers 95.2%, b/c 2.69 times, indirect awards up to 71.5%.

— Tokyo's core consumer price index (excludes fresh food) accelerates by 0.2pp to 3.6% year over year in April, fastest pace since January 2023. Headline unchanged at 3.4%.

— Spain's HICP slows to 1.9% year over year in April from 2.2% in May, below 2% target for first time since October. Core CPI dips to 2.1%.

— Day ahead: U.S. PCE index, SocGen forecasts 0.1% month over month for headline and core; personal income, SocGen forecasts: 0.4%, spending 0.3%. Federal Reserve speaker Daly. European Central Bank's Muller, Panetta and Vujcic. Germany's preliminary HICP. Q1 gross domestic product for Canada, Brazil and India. Poland's CPI.

— Nikkei -0.9%, EUR 10-year IRS unchanged at 2.50%, Brent crude -0.4% at $63.9/barrel, Gold -0.7% at 3,294/oz.

data.title: Xingqi Pharmaceutical Says No Adverse Effects Seen in Phase 1 Eye Drops Trial
data.description:
Shenyang Xingqi Pharmaceutical said there were no adverse effects seen in its phase 1 trial for the SQ-22031 eye drops for neurotrophic keratitis, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The product was safe and trial participants tolerated the product well, the filing sid.

data.title: Indian Navy will lead New Delhi's response to any future Pakistani aggression, minister says
data.description:
India will use the firepower of its navy in response to any future aggression by Pakistan, India's defence minister said on Friday, weeks after the fiercest fighting in decades between the two nuclear-armed neighbours.

Relations between India and Pakistan are tense after four days of fighting this month, which involved fighter jets, missiles, drones and artillery before a ceasefire was announced.

"If Pakistan resorts to anything evil or unethical, it will, this time, face the firepower and ire of the Indian Navy," Defence Minister Rajnath Singh said on the aircraft carrier INS Vikrant off the coast of the western Indian state of Goa.

A spokesperson for Pakistan's military referred Reuters to a May 12 statement, which said there would be a "comprehensive and decisive" response whenever Pakistan's sovereignty was "threatened and territorial integrity violated".

The latest fighting erupted after 26 people, mostly Indian tourists, were killed in an April 22 attack in Kashmir, a disputed Himalayan territory claimed by both nations. New Delhi blamed the attack on "terrorists" backed by Pakistan, which denied the charge.

A ceasefire took effect on May 10 and a top Pakistani military official told Reuters on Friday that both countries were close to reducing their troop build-up along their border to pre-conflict levels.

The Indian Navy has said it deployed its carrier battle group, submarines and other aviation assets in the northern Arabian Sea within 96 hours of the April 22 attack.

Defence Minister Singh said 'Operation Sindoor', under which India launched the strikes on Pakistan, was paused, but not yet over.

"We stopped our military actions on our own terms. Our forces had not even started showing their might," he said.

data.title: Polypropylene (PP) spot prices quote lower in Europe
data.description: This week, PP spot prices declined, while contract prices remained steady in the European region. An industry source in Europe informed a Polymerupdate team member, "The polypropylene (PP) market in Europe was subdued this week. Homopolymer injection-grade and copolymer polypropylene spot prices decreased throughout the week, as low demand and competitive imports continued to exert downward pressure on spot pricing. The demand for PP remained low, showing minimal purchasing activity observed in the spot market. At the same time, abundant supply also applied downward pressure on prices. On the supply side, imports of PP, especially homopolymer injection grades, from the Middle East and Asia-Pacific that arrived in Europe in May also added to the supply abundance and pessimism observed in recent weeks. Nonetheless, the market showed minimal interest in these proposals. Borealis announced a Eur100 million investment this week to create a new high melt strength PP production line at its facility in Burghausen, Germany. The upcoming line, expected to commence operations in the latter half of 2026, will increase Borealis' capacity to produce recyclable, high melt strength PP threefold, serving the consumer goods, automotive, and construction sectors. However, the relatively stable feedstock propylene's June monthly contract price (MCP) indicates that a potential short-term price floor could have been achieved in Europe. This has generated hope among certain sellers that a rebound in demand might occur in June, following a period of relatively unsatisfactory demand in May. However, purchasers continue to be cautious about committing to any volumes for June, even when presented with price rollovers, as they are in a wait-and-see stance, seeking more insight into the forecast. The source added, "The tariff environment continues to be dynamic. Worries about a rise in tariffs surfaced when US President Donald Trump suggested a 50% tariff on all imports from the EU starting 1 June. This stands in contrast to a recent thaw in trade tensions between the US and various nations, particularly China. Declines in stock markets were observed on both sides of the Atlantic, with certain downstream sectors possibly vulnerable to a significant decrease in demand for goods sent to the US, particularly in the automotive sector. In the near term, the unpredictability will pose a challenge for all to prepare for and handle. In the meantime, details about US-China trade talks were scarce. This has brought about a change in geopolitical sentiment, leading many market players to now anticipate that trade agreements will be finalized eventually. However, the question lingers on whether this could be accomplished within the designated 90-day pause, or if an extension might be necessary.

data.title: REG - DFI Retail Group - Divests Shares in Robinsons Retail
data.description:
RNS Number : 8053K DFI Retail Group Holdings Ltd 30 May 2025  

Announcement

The following announcement was issued today to a Regulatory Information Service approved by the Financial Conduct Authority in the United Kingdom.

DFI Retail Group Divests Shares in Robinsons Retail

30 May 2025 - DFI Retail Group Holdings Limited ('DFI' or the 'Group') today announces the sale of 315,309,310 common shares in Robinsons Retail Holdings, Inc. ('RRHI'), representing approximately 22.2% of RRHI's outstanding shares. This transaction reflects DFI's strategic pivot from a portfolio investor to a focused operating company, enabling the Group to divest minority positions and redeploy capital to support the growth and higher returns of subsidiary businesses.

DFI first became a significant minority shareholder in RRHI in 2018 through the share-for-share swap transaction involving Rustan Supercenters, Inc.

Following this divestment, the Group will review the use of the divestment proceeds to support its capital allocation strategy and long-term growth priorities which include - but not be limited to - expanding digital retail media, advancing own brand innovation, and enhancing omnichannel capabilities across its key markets.

DFI remains confident in RRHI's long-term prospects and the continued success of their exclusive distribution of Meadows and Guardian brands.

Scott Price, Group Chief Executive of DFI Retail Group, said, "We would like to sincerely thank the Robinsons Retail team for their hard work, partnership, and commitment over the years. Our collaboration has been instrumental in growing our presence in the Philippines, and we look forward to continuing this strong relationship as we each focus on our strategic priorities."

"This transaction represents a significant step in our evolution as an operating company, enabling us to redeploy capital to support growth and enhance shareholder returns across our subsidiary businesses. We will evaluate the deployment of divestment proceeds to ensure alignment with our capital allocation strategy and long-term growth ambitions."

The transaction was executed via a special block sale on the Philippine Stock Exchange, with pricing agreed upon based on prevailing market conditions and strategic considerations.

-Ends-

About DFI Retail Group

DFI Retail Group is a leading Asian retailer, driven by its purpose to 'Sustainably Serve Asia for Generations with Everyday Moments'.

As at 31 December 2024, the Group, its associates and joint ventures operated over 10,700 outlets, of which more than 5,000 stores were operated by subsidiaries. The Group, together with associates and joint ventures, employed over 190,000 people, with over 45,000 people employed by subsidiaries. The Group had total annual revenue in 2024 of US$24.9 billion and reported revenue of US$8.9 billion.

The Group is dedicated to delivering quality, value and service to Asian consumers through a compelling retail experience, supported by an extensive store network and highly efficient supply chains.

The Group, including associates and joint ventures, operates a portfolio of well-known brands across six key divisions: health and beauty, convenience, food, home furnishings, restaurants and other retailing.

The Group's parent company, DFI Retail Group Holdings Limited, is incorporated in Bermuda and has a primary listing in the equity shares (transition) category of the London Stock Exchange, with secondary listings in Bermuda and Singapore. The Group's businesses are managed from Hong Kong. DFI Retail Group is a member of the Jardine Matheson group.  

For more information, please contact: 

Karen Chan (Investor Relations)                                                       

+852 2299 1380

karen.chan@dfiretailgroup.com

Christine Chung (Corporate Communications and Affairs)  

+852 2299 1056

data.title: ContextVision, Amra Medical Partner for Liver Disease Detection Tech
data.description:
Medical technology software company ContextVision said Friday it entered a research collaboration with Sweden-based Amra Medical to develop multimodal imaging for liver disease diagnosis.

The partnership builds on ContextVision's ongoing data quality project, which aims to develop artificial intelligence-powered, organ-specific imaging to screen and quantify metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease.

Amra Medical will contribute its magnetic resonance imaging-based analysis to the initiative to facilitate correlations between MRI and ultrasound imaging biomarkers and enhance the dataset.

data.title: UBS ups Solaria to 'buy' on strong power demand driven by data centres and 'BESS'
data.description:
** UBS upgrades Solaria to "buy" from "neutral" as the market is overlooking the turning point driven by data centres and batteries/storage ('BESS') gaining momentum

** Solaria's shares up around 7% on Friday after surging 4.4% in the previous session

** "On top of that, Solaria has already secured 1.2GW of data centre connection points and we expect a first deal in H2, likely a powerful positive catalyst"- UBS

** Broker also sees solar market as the cheapest energy source and says the company is "the most competitive player in the market" in its view

** UBS expects Solaria to get about 10% market share and sees the company with 3.1GW in operation in 2025 in a scenario of zero growth

** Shares on track for best day since min-June 2024, when it closed 9.8% higher

** Including session's rise, stock has dropped 4.54% YTD​

data.title: Apparel maker Gap falls on warning of tariff hit to income
data.description:
** Shares of Old Navy-parent Gap slide about 15% to $23.81 premarket

** Apparel maker flags tariff-related costs in the range of $250 million to $300 million, but aims to mitigate more than half of that amount

** Says U.S. tariffs will pressure its annual operating income; maintains annual forecasts excluding that hit

** Expects fiscal 2025 sales to rise 1% to 2% and operating income growth of 8% to 10%

** GAP sourced less than 10% of its merchandise from China in 2024 - annual report

** Q1 revenue of $3.46 billion beats analysts' estimate of $3.42 billion - data compiled by LSEG

** Jefferies cuts PT on GAP to $26 from $29; says performance at brands remains a mixed bag and visibility into profit growth is limited

** As of last close, Gap shares up 18% so far this year

data.title: Malaysian Shares End Week in Red; Elsoft Research Drops 3%
data.description:
Malaysian shares ended in red on Friday, extending losses from the previous four sessions on profit-taking, and in line with regional losses.

The FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI, the main gauge of Malaysian stocks, shed 10.63 points, to end 0.70% lower at 1,508.35. The day range was between 1,508.35 and 1,522.06.

In economic news, Malaysia's headline inflation remained unchanged at 1.4% in April, while core inflation edged up to 2% from 1.9% in the preceding month, according to Bank Negara Malaysia.

In corporate news, ASM Automation Group (KLSE:ASM) targets to raise 30.9 million ringgit via its initial public offering on the Ace Market of Bursa Malaysia.

The IPO of the packaging automation systems involves a public issue of 128.3 million shares and an offer for sale of 53.5 million shares at 0.17 ringgit apiece.

Shares of Elsoft Research dropped about 3% after the company appointed Chuah Hean Teik as board chairman on Thursday.

Shares of Comfort Gloves dropped over 1% after Chief Financial Officer Leong Wai Leong stepped down under a mutual separation deal.

The Directors consider that in the absence of observable market data on its Russian investments resulting from the closure of the Moscow Exchange (MOEX) to overseas investors, there has been a material change to the market value of its Russian investments and therefore a fair value valuation methodology has been applied to those investments held. For its MOEX local stock, an alternative fair value adjustment has been applied to the last trade price on 25th February 2022. The price of these stocks has been determined by taking the live market price as at 25th February 2022 and applying a 99% provision for valuation. Similarly, for the American Depositary Receipts and Global Depositary Receipts an alternative fair value adjustment has been applied to the last trade price on 2nd March 2022 and a 99% provision for valuation applied.

data.description:

FORM 8.3

PUBLIC OPENING POSITION DISCLOSURE/DEALING DISCLOSURE BY

A PERSON WITH INTERESTS IN RELEVANT SECURITIES REPRESENTING 1% OR MORE

Rule 8.3 of the Takeover Code (the "Code")

1.         KEY INFORMATION

(a) Full name of discloser:

Redmayne Bentley LLP

(b) Owner or controller of interests and short positions disclosed, if different from 1(a):

     The naming of nominee or vehicle companies is insufficient.  For a trust, the trustee(s), settlor and beneficiaries must be named.

(c) Name of offeror/offeree in relation to whose relevant securities this form relates:

     Use a separate form for each offeror/offeree

Primary Health Properties PLC

(d) If an exempt fund manager connected with an offeror/offeree, state this and specify identity of offeror/offeree:

(e) Date position held/dealing undertaken:

     For an opening position disclosure, state the latest practicable date prior to the disclosure

29th May 2025

(f)  In addition to the company in 1(c) above, is the discloser making disclosures in respect of any other party to the offer?

     If it is a cash offer or possible cash offer, state "N/A"

N/A

2.         POSITIONS OF THE PERSON MAKING THE DISCLOSURE

If there are positions or rights to subscribe to disclose in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(c), copy table 2(a) or (b) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security.

Where there have been dealings in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(c), copy table 3(a), (b), (c) or (d) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security dealt in.

4.         OTHER INFORMATION

(a)        Indemnity and other dealing arrangements

  • Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the person making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:

    Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included.  If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

(b)        Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives

  • Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the person making the disclosure and any other person relating to:

data.title: Swiss private bank Julius Baer names new South East Asia market heads
data.description:
Switzerland-based private bank Julius Baer said on Friday it has appointed Shui Wei Ho and Vi Sun Yang as market heads for South East Asia, effective July 1.

Shui Wei and Yang will replace Chin Lit Yee, who is retiring after nearly a decade at the bank.

Yang took on the role as Group Head South East Asia in July 2022, while Shui Wei joined in the same role a month later.

Both Shui Wei and Yang will remain based in Singapore and report directly to Jimmy Lee, the region head Asia and member of the global wealth management committee, the bank said.

data.title: Readly International to Delist from Nasdaq First North Growth Market
data.description:
Readly International said Friday that Nasdaq First North Growth Market approved its delisting plan.

The delisting, with the last day of trading set to June 30, follows Tidnings AB Marieberg's acquisition of over 90% of the Swedish digital media company.

data.title: Li Auto's Total Sales Remain Challenging in 2025 — Market Talk
data.description: Li Auto's 2025 total sales remain challenging, Nomura analysts write in a note. The company's first-quarter earnings were largely in line with expectations and it and guided for 123,000-128,000 units of total shipments for the second quarter. With Li Auto's L series updates and efforts on promotions, the company should be able to maintain existing sales volumes despite an increase in the number of competitive new models in the market, they say. However, since the company doesn't have a plan for new hybrid plug-in models this year, the brokerage remains cautious on Li Auto's 2025 shipment growth. If the new full-electric SUVs are welcomed by the market, that could be a major growth opportunity for the company by the end of this year, Nomura says.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Sanofi drops as lung disease therapy fails to meet primary endpoint
data.description:
** Shares in Sanofi drop 5% as it announces results of its experimental therapy for a lung condition

** AERIFY-2, one of two therapies being developed by the world's largest vaccine makers by sales, did not meet the primary endpoint in its COPD phase 3 studies

** JPM notes that while these results are "disappointing," they ultimately "expect the product to reach the market, albeit with a 2-3 year delay"

** Shares in the company fell 4.1% in the last month​, underperforming the STOXX index, which rose 3.9%

data.title: Wells Fargo Adjusts Price Target on Ulta Beauty to $350 From $315, Maintains Underweight Rating
data.description: Ulta Beauty has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $463.78, according to analysts polled by FactSet.
